Writer (installer) and stripper (uninstaller) share byte-identical contracts: CLAUDE.md <!-- DMC:BEGIN -->/<!-- DMC:END -->, .gitignore # DMC:BEGIN/# DMC:END (distinct, valid gitignore comments), sentinel '# DMC-CREATED', receipt {created_paths,merged_targets}. strip_markers removes the marker block PLUS contiguous blank separators before/after, byte-preserving the rest; byte-clean holds on canonical-form fixtures (5-fixture diff -r + porcelain empty, re-run above). The created-.codex test asserts the sentinel bytes == '# DMC-CREATED'.

harness-matrix.json Codex column is entirely 'advisory'/asymmetry with a named backstop each — direct inspection of all 8 rows found NO forbidden lexeme (enforced|enforce|fires|firing|runtime-enforced|active|guaranteed); the claude-code 'enforced' cells never contain the token 'codex' so per-host rendering keeps them out of the /codex/i scope. Manifest labels the Codex adapter ADVISORY.

All four disclosed residuals are honest and correctly framed, none hiding a gap: (1) the verify-crosscheck REFUSE (exit 3) on the two .harness/plans/* is recorded VERBATIM and correctly explained as a pre-run-artifact suspend/wait-state hold — it is NOT a plan Verification Command, so it fails no required plan criterion, and the report explicitly declines to game it (declaring/committing would be a human gate); (2) 0644 fixture-script mode is cosmetic — every real caller uses `bash <script>` (I re-ran all four that way); (3) canonical-form-only byte restore is honestly SEMANTIC for non-canonical hosts; (4) the A1 dmc-* fallback trade-off is a documented residual.
